description: Generate wrapper APIs that detect the installed Serilog version at runtime and adapt behavior accordingly. Uses compile-time #if directives for framework-dependent features and runtime checks for optional APIs.

mappings.description: Core logging methods — identical across all versions
mappings.notes: No version branching needed. API surface identical.

mappings.description: CloseAndFlush — sync in v2, prefer async in v3+
mappings.notes: Wrapper calls CloseAndFlushAsync() on v3+, falls back to Task.Run(() => CloseAndFlush()) on v2.
Wrapper exposes nullable TraceId/SpanId. Returns null on v2.x where properties don't exist.
On v4, uses native BatchingOptions. On v3, uses IBatchedLogEventSink directly. On v2, wraps with PeriodicBatchingSink NuGet.

mappings.description: Conditional enrichment — v3.1+, custom enricher on v2
mappings.notes: v3.1+ has native When(). v2 requires a custom ILogEventEnricher that evaluates the condition.
v4 uses case-insensitive matching. Wrapper normalizes source context names to avoid behavioral drift between versions.

mappings.description: Sink with LevelSwitch — overload added in v3
mappings.notes: LoggingLevelSwitch overload not available in v2. Wrapper ignores levelSwitch on v2.
New static utility in v4. Wrapper provides a polyfill on v2/v3.

versionBranch.default: // polyfill: manually build sink from configuration

unsupportedTransformations.description: LogEvent constructor with trace IDs — cannot polyfill on v2
unsupportedTransformations.source: new LogEvent(timestamp, level, exception, template, properties, traceId, spanId)
unsupportedTransformations.reason: The 7-parameter LogEvent constructor only exists in v3.1+. Cannot create LogEvents with embedded trace context on v2.
unsupportedTransformations.workaround: On v2, create the standard 5-parameter LogEvent and add TraceId/SpanId as regular properties via AddOrUpdateProperty.

unsupportedTransformations.description: Dotted property names — experimental v4 only
unsupportedTransformations.reason: AppContext switch-gated experimental feature in v4. Cannot be polyfilled on earlier versions.
unsupportedTransformations.workaround: Use flat property names across all versions.
